primary_accounting_issue: Éligibilité, en comptes consolidés, du risque de change sur un dividende intragroupe comptabilisé en créance à recevoir comme élément couvert dans une relation de couverture

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule to overlapping hedge-accounting guidance and selected IAS 39 because it is the only document in the provided context that directly governs hedged-item eligibility and the named hedge accounting models; IAS 21 expressly excludes hedge accounting from its scope and therefore does not compete as the governing lineage for this issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the context directly points to IAS 39 for hedge-accounting eligibility, while IAS 21 only provides supporting foreign-currency consolidation context.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern what can be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements and identify the named hedge accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify that IAS 21 does not govern hedge accounting and explain when intragroup monetary items create exchange differences not fully eliminated on consolidation.
This Interpretation supports the identification of net investment hedging as a distinct hedge accounting model and clarifies group-level foreign currency hedge mechanics.

peripheral_authority.reason: This addresses foreign currency effects for insurance contracts, not hedged-item eligibility for intragroup dividends in consolidated financial statements.
These paragraphs concern presentation, dividends on financial instruments and offsetting, not hedge-accounting eligibility of intragroup dividend receivables.
This standard concerns related party disclosures rather than hedge accounting treatment.
This standard addresses cash flow statement classification and financing liability reconciliations, not hedge-accounting eligibility.
This standard concerns current and deferred tax consequences, not foreign currency hedge designation of intragroup dividends.
This standard addresses provisions and contingent liabilities, not hedge accounting.
This paragraph concerns business combination measurement of insurance contracts, unrelated to the issue.
This standard addresses government grants and loans, not hedge-accounting eligibility.
This standard concerns employee benefits, unrelated to the issue.
This standard concerns hyperinflationary reporting and translation, not hedge designation of intragroup dividends.
This standard concerns retirement benefit plans, unrelated to the issue.
This standard contains disclosure simplifications and related-party disclosure requirements, not hedge-accounting eligibility.
This interpretation concerns changes in tax status, unrelated to the issue.
This interpretation addresses non-cash distributions to owners, not hedge accounting for intragroup dividend receivables.
This interpretation concerns members' shares and liability/equity classification, unrelated to the issue.

treatment_families.family: Named hedge accounting models available for a qualifying hedging relationship
Consolidated-financial-statements eligibility of intragroup foreign currency exposures as hedged items
Group-level foreign currency hedging of net investments in foreign operations

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct named hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct named hedge accounting model for exposures to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 identifies hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate named hedge accounting model, further supported by IFRIC 16.
